Septons CRE signal recorder workflow

reaches DON consensus on confidence/platform/direction, and writes
signed reports to SignalRegistry.sol on Ethereum Sepolia.

fix(ci): use shell: true for npm spawn to fix Windows EINVAL

Node.js 22 (CVE-2024-27980) forbids spawning .cmd files without
shell option. Replace platform-specific npmCommand() with plain "npm"
and shell: true so the shell resolves npm → npm.cmd on Windows.

commit.message:
fix: TG cross-court dedup + remove dead sg_gerichte scraper

Add TG overlap group to _COURT_OVERLAP_GROUPS and strip "Nr." noise
word from docket normalization so entscheidsuche RBOG 2024 Nr. 10
matches direct scraper RBOG-2024-10 (~2,200 duplicates).

Delete sg_gerichte.py — Weblaw host DNS retired, sg_publikationen
is the active replacement.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix roaming state to use reactive state instead of ref-based consumption

The roaming state from server-side session claim arrives async (after page
components mount), so the old consumeRoamingState ref approach missed the
timing window. Now uses reactive state (roamingState/clearRoamingState) so
pages can react via useEffect when the claim resolves.

Also prevents saving stale state or syncing URL while roaming restore is pending.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add trends UI, nightly scheduler, and data freshness indicator (#106)

* Enforce all test layers in CI and add Definition of Done

Add Jest CI workflow (jest.yml) running lint + unit/integration/component
tests on every PR and before deploy. Update deploy.yml to gate on both
Jest and Playwright in parallel. Add test:ci script, exclude worktree
paths from Jest, fix outdated Playwright status in docs, and codify a
Definition of Done checklist in CLAUDE.md.

Closes #96 (branch protection setup tracked separately)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove lint step from jest.yml to fix CI failure

ESLint 10.0.2 resolved via npm ci is incompatible with
eslint-plugin-react from eslint-config-next (getFilename error).
Lint was never in CI before — remove it from the Jest workflow
and track the ESLint compatibility fix separately.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Pin ESLint to 9.39.3 and restore lint step in CI

The caret range allowed npm ci to resolve ESLint 10.0.2, which is
incompatible with eslint-plugin-react from eslint-config-next. Pin
to exact version 9.39.3 and restore the lint step in jest.yml.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix inaccurate 'serial' claim in test:ci comment

The --ci flag does not force serial execution — it changes snapshot
behavior and disables watch mode. Remove misleading description.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add "Previous Month" date range option to time tracking

Adds a "PM" button to the time range selector so users can review the
full previous month's data — useful on the 1st of the month when MTD
resets and 14d doesn't cover all of last month.

Closes #98

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Close remaining gaps in historical data layer (#66)

Add source column to snapshot tables, fix scheduler_log schema,
add has*Today() dedup guards to API routes, switch Docker to named volumes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Potential fix for code scanning alert no. 1: Workflow does not contain permissions

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <62310815+github-advanced-security[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

* Potential fix for code scanning alert no. 3: Workflow does not contain permissions

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <62310815+github-advanced-security[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address CodeRabbit review feedback

- Narrow ALTER TABLE catch blocks to only ignore "duplicate column" errors
- Add least-privilege permissions (contents: read) to jest.yml and deploy.yml
- Fix dateRange test to actually test a 30-day month (April, not May)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add cache-first read layer for team PR and time tracking APIs

Check SQLite for today's snapshot before hitting ADO/7Pace. Fall back
to stale cache (up to 7 days) when upstream APIs fail. Responses now
include a data_source field indicating origin (live/cache/cache-stale).

- Add DataSourceMeta type and extend TeamSummaryApiResponse + TeamTimeData
- Create lib/cache-utils.ts with isSnapshotFresh, buildDateRange, findMissingDates
- Add 5 read functions to lib/snapshots.ts (getTeamSnapshot, getTeamSnapshotRange,
  getTimeSnapshot, getTimeSnapshotRange, checkTeamCoverage)
- Use _team_response_ sentinel row for full TeamTimeData cache in time_tracking_snapshots
- Wire cache-first + stale-fallback logic in both API routes
- Add tests for cache-utils, snapshot reads, and API cache behavior (326 pass)

Closes #67

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address CodeRabbit review feedback on cache layer

- Make cache range-aware: verify cached period.days matches requested
  range before serving from cache (both PR and time tracking routes)
- Fix corrupt JSON handling: safeJsonParse null now returns cache miss
  instead of wrapping null in a truthy object; range readers skip
  corrupt rows and try next-most-recent
- Enrich checkTeamCoverage return type to { covered, total, complete }
- Add tests: range mismatch falls through to live, malformed JSON
  treated as cache miss (332 tests pass)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add trends UI, nightly scheduler, and data freshness indicator (#68)

- Trend charts (PR velocity, alignment score) on Team tab, open by default
- Sprint-over-sprint comparison cards with delta badges
- Weekly hours chart on Time Tracking tab
- Nightly scheduler via node-cron + instrumentation.ts (2 AM UTC)
- Manual scheduler trigger at /api/admin/scheduler/run
- Scheduler history table in Debug tab
- Data freshness indicator in dashboard header
- Trend API routes: /api/trends/team-pr, sprint-comparison, time-hours
- Pass scheduler env vars in docker-compose.prod.yml
- Deploy script prompts for ADO credentials and scheduler config
- Lower chart threshold to show data with any snapshots available

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add 5 Claude Code agents for Teachly development workflow

Adds specialised agents under .claude/agents/:
- code-reviewer: NestJS/Next.js correctness, tenant isolation, guard coverage
- db-architect: Prisma schema design, index patterns, migration safety
- feature-architect: Full feature design from data model to frontend components
- performance-optimizer: N+1 detection, cron efficiency, caching strategy
- security-reviewer: OWASP checks, auth/session, IDOR, file upload, tenant isolation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: patch Ruby configure Info.plist for Xcode 26 LDFLAGS check

Ruby 4.0.1 configure creates an Info.plist with empty CFBundleIdentifier
during LDFLAGS validation. Xcode 26 linker warns about this, and Ruby's
-Werror mode turns the warning into an error. Fix by patching configure
to use a valid bundle ID.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Prevent NaN/Infinity in REAL columns to fix Turso JSON parse failure

Guard all write boundaries with safeFloat() to prevent non-finite values
from being stored in REAL columns, which causes Bun's Response.json() to
fail on macOS. Adds migration 0020 to clean any existing poisoned rows.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Three Pillars document

- Result<T, E> as fundamental building block (not just a monad)
- Option<T> for explicit absence (not nullable)
- Unit for explicit completion (not void)
- Why these are necessities, not functional patterns

commit.message:
perf: split dashboard query — fast part always loads, review_stats with fallback

- Split getDashboardStats into 2 parallel queries
- Fast: stores + products + chat_stats via review_chat_links (<1s)
- Slow: review_stats (3.2M reviews) — runs in parallel, falls back to 0 on timeout
- Dashboard always loads instantly, review section fills in when ready

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(strongs): resolve OSIS format mismatch in verse study Strong's lookup

The Strong's word list wasn't showing on the Verse Study page because the
OSIS code was constructed from the long-form osis_code (e.g. "Leviticus.27.14")
instead of the short form stored in verse_keys (e.g. "Lev.27.14"). Now fetches
the correct short OSIS from verse_keys via kjv_verse_id before querying
get_strongs_for_osis_refs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use total RAM instead of available for model selection

Available RAM fluctuates based on running processes, causing false
fallback to the 4B model on 16GB machines. Total RAM is stable and
matches the actual hardware capability.

commit.message:
fix: zone auto-detection using TEXT grid labels instead of entity coords

Entity coordinates are paper-space (scaled), causing wrong zone detection.
Now parses X=/Y= grid labels from TEXT/MTEXT entities to get real-world
coordinates for accurate zone determination. Falls back to entity coords
if no grid labels found.

commit.message:
perf: normalise squash strings at load time to eliminate repeated allocations (#753) (#761)

* perf: normalise squash strings at load time to eliminate repeated allocations (#753)

Normalise NeuronJson.squash to ASCII uppercase during serde deserialisation
so downstream detection modules can match directly without per-neuron
.to_ascii_uppercase() allocations. Removes 20 redundant allocations from
11 modules, achieving 90-94% speedup in detection benchmarks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: auto-increment versions for changed projects

* Update Cargo.toml

commit.message:
perf: use cached store columns + dashboard cache + review_stats optimization

- getStores(): use s.total_reviews/s.total_chats/s.chat_tag_counts directly (skip 3.2M+444K scans)
- getDashboardStats(): 5-min in-memory cache for expensive dashboard query
- review_stats CTE: start from complaint_products (~500) then lookup reviews by index (vs scanning 3.2M)
- statement_timeout: 30s → 60s (cache protects from repeated slow queries)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add PK zones (NPK/CPK), new zones, who table, whois command, and numpad remap

Implement zone-based PvP with Shadowfen as NPK (instant respawn on death) and
Frozen Peaks as CPK (60-tick death timer, corpse drops inventory/gold). PK zones
override toggle_pvp for both player and bot attacks. Zone entry requires y/n
confirmation. Prompt shows <NPK>/<CPK> indicator. Track NPK/CPK wins/losses on
players and bots.

Add Shadowfen Marsh (10 rooms, levels 6-12) and Frozen Peaks (10 rooms, levels
25-40) zones with mobs, maps, and data files.

Rework the who command into an aligned table with Name, Level, Class, PK Record,
and Status columns. Color-code levels by range, abbreviate race/class to 3 chars,
show total levels for multiclassed players, and display dead bots with DEAD flag.

Add whois command to inspect a bot player's full stats, equipment, skills, and
location. Add summon debug command to teleport bots for testing.

Remap numpad: 9=up, 3=down, 7=inventory, 1=who (removed diagonal directions).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: traffic_limit now compares total allocated data, not consumed traffic

Previously remaining_traffic used users_usage (consumed bytes = 0 on fresh
installs), so any user with data_limit < traffic_limit could always be created,
making the budget check useless.

Now checks against the SUM of all existing users' data_limits under the admin,
so 8GB + 4GB = 12GB correctly exceeds a 10GB traffic_limit.

Also fixes modify_user to exclude the current user from the sum.

Additionally: merge 15 divergent Alembic migration heads into one so that
'alembic upgrade head' no longer fails; change Dockerfile from ';' to '&&'
so the app won't silently start with an unapplied schema.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: disable Astro i18n to allow custom language detection

- Remove Astro's built-in i18n config that was overriding our redirect
- Now root path uses client-side JavaScript for language detection
